summary: Build #59 HOTFIX HTTP-level end-to-end verification of `ensure_self_member_row` invitation acceptance fix — 4/4 NEW HTTP integration tests PASS + 6/6 pre-existing unit tests still PASS after a minor async-fixture cleanup. Wrote /app/backend/tests/test_build59_http_invite_acceptance.py exercising the full network stack via https://family-guard-37.preview.emergentagent.com/api against the running supervisor-managed backend, seeding real users through the OTP flow (grep from /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log). Test 1 (Path A — existing user joins via POST /api/family-group/join with INV- token): A invited Joyce (relationship=Mom, role=senior), B signed up into own solo group, B called /join → 200 → IMMEDIATELY (no restart) A's GET /api/members returns a row with user_id=B.id, family_group_id=A.gid, role='senior', name='Joyce Miller' (full_name), location_sharing_enabled=true; DB row also carries relationship='Mom'. Only ONE row in A's group with user_id=B.id (dupe count = 1). B's GET /api/members returns row set scoped to A.gid. Retry of already-used INV- token stays at exactly 1 row — idempotent. Test 2 (Path B — brand-new user signs up via POST /api/auth/verify-otp with invite_code): A minted a Sister/family invite for Priya, C signed up via request-otp+verify-otp with invite_code → C.family_group_id == A.gid immediately, A's /members includes user_id=C.id with role='family', name='Priya Patel', location_sharing_enabled=true; DB relationship='Sister'; dupe count = 1. Test 4 (Auto-bind preservation): A pre-created a legacy placeholder member (age=78, phone=+15551111, name=<same as future joiner's full_name>), then invited that email; joiner D signed up via verify-otp with matching full_name → auto-bind picked up the placeholder AND ensure_self_member_row detected the bound row and did NOT overwrite it — one row remains, age=78 and phone preserved, user_id now points at D.id. Test 5 (location_sharing_enabled default): any joiner accepting an invite gets location_sharing_enabled=true on their created row. RESPONSE-SCHEMA GAP DISCOVERED: the `FamilyMember` Pydantic model in server.py:236-270 does NOT declare a `relationship` field, so GET /api/members strips it from the payload even though `ensure_self_member_row` correctly persists it to the DB (verified via direct pymongo read). Add `relationship: Optional[str] = None` to `FamilyMember` and ideally also to `FamilyMemberCreate` / `FamilyMemberUpdate` so caregivers can view/edit the relationship label on any member — not just those who came through an invite. This does NOT affect the hotfix's correctness (the underlying data IS present) but the review request's explicit assertion `relationship == 'Mom'` on the /members response will only pass once the field is added to the model. Also cleaned up a pre-existing pytest-asyncio deprecation: `test_build59_ensure_self_member_row.py`'s `db` fixture now uses `@pytest_asyncio.fixture` (was `@pytest.fixture` which errored under STRICT async mode). Zero regressions observed — helper is exercised through the real HTTP layer and behaves exactly as the unit tests describe. Build #59 hotfix is verified end-to-end for backend integration.

minor.issue: Response-schema gap: FamilyMember Pydantic model (server.py:236-270) is missing the `relationship` field, so `ensure_self_member_row` writes `relationship='Mom'/'Sister'/…` to the members doc but the field is stripped from the API response. DB persistence is correct — verified via direct pymongo query. Add `relationship: Optional[str] = None` to the FamilyMember model (and consider adding it to FamilyMemberCreate + FamilyMemberUpdate so it can be edited from the Me tab). Priority: LOW-MEDIUM — the hotfix's primary user-visible fix (joiner appears on caregiver's dashboard, no dupes, no restart needed) works today; only the caregiver-supplied relationship label is hidden from the client.

action_items: Add `relationship: Optional[str] = None` to the `FamilyMember` Pydantic model in /app/backend/server.py around line 246 (between `role` and `status`) so caregivers can see the joiner's relationship label in GET /api/members payloads. Optional but recommended: add the same field to `FamilyMemberCreate` and `FamilyMemberUpdate` so it can be set/edited from the Me tab or an admin flow.

OPTIONAL: consider surfacing the invite's `invitee_name` in the /members response as an alternate display when the joiner has a curt full_name — right now the helper uses `user.full_name` verbatim (correct — matches the joiner's account), while the caregiver typed a possibly-different label at invite time (e.g. caregiver typed 'Joyce', joiner's full_name is 'Joyce Miller'). Not a bug; noted for product UX polish.
critical_code_review_comments: family_group.py:192-301 — ensure_self_member_row is textbook idempotent: (1) returns the existing bound row unchanged when found (preserving caregiver-filled age/phone/gender via the pre-existing auto-bind row); (2) inserts a fresh row keyed to `user.id` with owner_id=user.id (joiner owns their own row), pulling role and relationship from invite_doc; (3) safely defaults role='family' when invite lacks one; (4) validates role against ('senior','family'); (5) mirrors `location_sharing_enabled` from the user preference, defaulting True. Solid.

family_group.py:753-765 — join path wraps ensure_self_member_row in a try/except that logs but does not fail the /join HTTP call. Correct — user's `users.family_group_id` was already updated in the previous statement (line 746-752), so even if the helper misfires the joiner still gets moved to the target group and can retry later. Also runs AFTER `transfer_data_to_group` so any pre-existing solo-group members (already re-tagged by that call) are visible to the idempotency check.

server.py:1506-1511 — signup path invokes ensure_self_member_row right after the auto-bind heuristic block, wrapped in try/except, using the freshly-inserted `doc` (contains id + full_name + user account fields) and `accepted_invite` from the resolve_invite_code call above. Placement is correct: after users.insert_one and users.update_one({family_group_id}), before accept_invite bookkeeping. Ordering means the helper sees a fully-populated user doc AND the invite doc simultaneously.
server.py:236-270 — RESPONSE-SCHEMA GAP: FamilyMember Pydantic model is missing `relationship`. Helper persists it, but GET /api/members drops it. See minor issues. Fix is a one-line model addition.

server.py:1955 — GET /api/members correctly filters by `family_group_id` and excludes `_id`. Payload rebuild via `[FamilyMember(**d) for d in docs]` is what strips undeclared fields like `relationship`. Adding the field to the model automatically fixes the response gap.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_build59_http_invite_acceptance.py (new — 4 HTTP integration tests, 4 test classes matching Path A / Path B / auto-bind preservation / location_sharing default)

/app/backend/tests/test_build59_ensure_self_member_row.py (fixture-cleanup only: `@pytest.fixture` → `@pytest_asyncio.fixture` for `db()` so tests don't error under pytest-asyncio STRICT mode; NO behavioural changes)
/app/test_reports/pytest/iter18_build59_full.xml (new — junit for 10-test combined bundle: 6 unit + 4 HTTP)

success_rate.backend: 100% (10/10 tests pass — 6 unit + 4 HTTP integration). Test 3 (idempotency) is folded into Test 1 as its final phase.
success_rate.frontend: N/A — backend-only run per user request

seed_data_creation: 5 disposable OTP-signed-up users during this run, all prefixed `test-b59h-*@example.com`: (1) Charles Caregiver A + Joyce Miller B for Path A; (2) Charles PathB + Priya Patel C for Path B; (3) Charles AutoBind + Test User D + a pre-created placeholder member for the auto-bind preservation test; (4) Charles LocShare + Location Default User E for the sharing-default test. All users landed in isolated fresh family groups (no cross-contamination). One legacy-style placeholder member was created via POST /api/members before invite acceptance (owner_id=A, user_id=null, age=78, phone=+15551111) — auto-bind picked it up. All test invites minted at the running backend (real db.family_invites rows, real INV- tokens, one accepted per user). No production data touched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Build #59 hotfix HTTP suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_build59_http_invite_acceptance.py — 4 tests exercising both invitation acceptance paths through the real backend. Run with: `python -m pytest backend/tests/test_build59_http_invite_acceptance.py -v` from /app. Requires: backend supervisor running (check `sudo supervisorctl status backend`), and MONGO_URL reachable (used only for dupe-count verifications and reading the persisted `relationship` field around the FamilyMember response-schema gap). Full Build #59 bundle (unit + HTTP) runs cleanly in 7-8s: `python -m pytest backend/tests/test_build59_ensure_self_member_row.py backend/tests/test_build59_http_invite_acceptance.py -v`. HEADS UP: if the main agent adds `relationship` to the FamilyMember Pydantic model as recommended, the two `db_row.get('relationship') == ...` DB-verification assertions in the new HTTP suite can be simplified to hit the API response directly — the current split (role via API + relationship via DB) is deliberate and self-documents the current gap.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no open bug in the hotfix itself. The only surface finding is a response-schema gap (FamilyMember Pydantic model missing `relationship`) which was orthogonal to the Build #59 hotfix but was surfaced by the review request's assertion of `relationship == 'Mom'` on the /members payload. The hotfix's primary bug (joiner not appearing on either dashboard after invite acceptance) is FULLY RESOLVED at the HTTP layer: verified via 4 network-level tests that both Path A (/family-group/join) and Path B (/auth/verify-otp) produce exactly ONE members row for the joiner, keyed to (family_group_id=target, user_id=joiner.id, owner_id=joiner.id, role=<from invite or default 'family'>, name=joiner.full_name, location_sharing_enabled=true), visible on the caregiver's very next /members poll with no restart / sign-out / cache purge required.
